The country experiences its hottest period from January to April, with temperatures often reaching 28.5 degrees Celsius, while the rainiest months occur from January to March, with an average of 30 rainy days, making May to October the best season to relocate or visit for comfortable weather, with slightly cooler temperatures and fewer rainy days. For expats and digital nomads seeking an idyllic island getaway, the dry season from May to October is the perfect time to immerse themselves in Wallis and Futuna's lush landscapes, pristine beaches, and vibrant culture, with average temperatures ranging from 27.7 to 28.4 degrees Celsius and fewer than 29 rainy days per month.
countries.health_summary: Wallis and Futuna offers a unique lifestyle with its tropical climate and natural beauty, though specific data on healthcare quality metrics like life expectancy, medical doctors per 10k people, adult obesity rates, and out-of-pocket health expenditure are not available. The island's remoteness may influence healthcare infrastructure and accessibility, making it essential for potential residents or visitors to research local health services thoroughly.

countries.name: Yemen
countries.climate_summary: Yemen's climate is characterized as a scorching hot desert type, with temperatures often soaring to sweltering highs, particularly during the summer months of June to August, which also coincidentally experience a lull in rainfall, while the months of April and August are the rainiest, with the latter being the wettest. The country's hottest period typically occurs in June, with average maximum temperatures reaching a sizzling 28.69°C, while the coolest months are from December to February, with mild temperatures ranging from 22°C to 8°C. For expats and digital nomads seeking a comfortable climate, the best season to relocate or visit Yemen would be during the spring months of September to November or March to May, when the weather is pleasantly warm and dry, with average temperatures ranging from 22°C to 27°C, making it an ideal time to explore the country's rich cultural heritage and breathtaking landscapes.
countries.health_summary: Healthcare quality and lifestyle in Yemen present a mixed picture. With a life expectancy of 65.8 years, the country faces significant challenges in public health. The ratio of medical doctors to people is just 1.6 per 10k, indicating limited access to healthcare services. Additionally, 13.3% of adults are obese, highlighting issues related to lifestyle and dietary habits.

countries.name: Zambia

countries.climate_summary: Zambia boasts a savanna climate, characterized by warm temperatures and distinct wet and dry seasons, with the hottest period occurring from September to November, when average maximum temperatures soar to over 28 degrees Celsius, and the rainiest months being from January to March, with an average of 27 rainy days. The country's wettest season, marked by frequent downpours and high humidity, gives way to a dry and mild winter from June to August, with average temperatures ranging from 12 to 22 degrees Celsius, making it the ideal time to relocate or visit for comfortable weather. During this dry season, digital nomads and expats can enjoy pleasant days and cool nights, perfect for exploring Zambia's stunning natural wonders, from the Victoria Falls to the South Luangwa National Park, without the sweltering heat and torrential rains that dominate the rest of the year.
countries.health_summary: Zambia faces challenges in healthcare with a life expectancy of 61.0 years, reflecting potential issues in medical resources and access. There is one medical doctor available for every ten thousand people, indicating a shortage in healthcare professionals. Additionally, 11.6% of adults are classified as obese, highlighting lifestyle factors that could be influenced by the country's healthcare quality and services.

The rainiest months are typically from January to March, with an average of 20-27 rainy days, while the dry season from May to August brings mild and pleasant weather, making it the ideal time to relocate or visit for digital nomads and expats seeking comfortable temperatures and minimal rainfall. For an unforgettable experience, the best season to visit or relocate to Zimbabwe is from May to August, when the climate is mild and sunny, with average temperatures ranging from 20-23 degrees Celsius, making it perfect for outdoor activities and exploration.

countries.name: Åland Islands
countries.climate_summary: The Åland Islands boast a humid continental climate, characterized by warm summers and icy winters, with the hottest period of the year unfolding in July, when average maximum temperatures soar to 20.13°C, while the rainiest months occur in August, November, and December, with an average of 20 rainy days. Expats and digital nomads seeking a comfortable climate should consider relocating or visiting during the spring and early summer months, from May to June, when the weather is mild and pleasant, with average temperatures ranging from 12.09°C to 17.61°C, and fewer rainy days. With its unique blend of Nordic charm and maritime influences, the Åland Islands offer a captivating destination for those who prefer a temperate climate, with the best time to experience the islands' natural beauty and outdoor activities being during the spring and early summer, when the landscape is in full bloom and the days are warm and sunny.
